hawkish_phrases: risks of easing policy too quickly, thereby possibly hindering further progress on inflation
the Committee could pause its easing of the policy rate and hold it at a restrictive level if inflation remained elevated
uncertainties concerning the level of the neutral rate of interest complicated the assessment of the degree of restrictiveness of monetary policy
dovish_phrases: Almost all participants judged that, though month-to-month movements would remain volatile, incoming data generally remained consistent with inflation returning sustainably to 2 percent
waning business pricing power, the Committee's still-restrictive monetary policy stance, and well-anchored longer-term inflation expectations
Almost all participants judged the risks to the attainment of their dual-mandate objectives of maximum employment and price stability to be roughly in balance
it would likely be appropriate to move gradually toward a more neutral stance of policy over time
summary: The Committee unanimously cut rates by 25 basis points to 4.5-4.75%, with balanced risk assessment and guidance toward gradual further easing, though the statement removed language about greater confidence in disinflation progress.
